verses.latin_vulgate: Osculetur me osculo oris sui ;  quia meliora sunt ubera tua vino,
verses.king_james: The song of songs, which is Solomon’s.

verses.latin_vulgate: Osculetur me osculo oris sui ;  quia meliora sunt ubera tua vino,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יִשָּׁקֵ֙נִי֙ מִנְּשִׁיק֣וֹת פִּ֔יהוּ כִּֽי־טוֹבִ֥ים דֹּדֶ֖יךָ מִיָּֽיִן׃
verses.king_james: Let him kiss me with the kisses of his mouth: for thy love is better than wine.

verses.latin_vulgate: fragrantia unguentis optimis.  Oleum effusum nomen tuum ;  ideo adolescentulæ dilexerunt te.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לְרֵ֙יחַ֙ שְׁמָנֶ֣יךָ טוֹבִ֔ים שֶׁ֖מֶן תּוּרַ֣ק שְׁמֶ֑ךָ עַל־כֵּ֖ן עֲלָמ֥וֹת אֲהֵבֽוּךָ׃
verses.king_james: Because of the savour of thy good ointments thy name is as ointment poured forth, therefore do the virgins love thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Trahe me, post te curremus  in odorem unguentorum tuorum.  Introduxit me rex in cellaria sua ;  exsultabimus et lætabimur in te,  memores uberum tuorum super vinum.  Recti diligunt te.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מׇשְׁכֵ֖נִי אַחֲרֶ֣יךָ נָּר֑וּצָה הֱבִיאַ֨נִי הַמֶּ֜לֶךְ חֲדָרָ֗יו נָגִ֤ילָה וְנִשְׂמְחָה֙ בָּ֔ךְ נַזְכִּ֤ירָה דֹדֶ֙יךָ֙ מִיַּ֔יִן מֵישָׁרִ֖ים אֲהֵבֽוּךָ׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: Draw me, we will run after thee: the king hath brought me into his chambers: we will be glad and rejoice in thee, we will remember thy love more than wine: the upright love thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nigra sum, sed formosa, filiæ Jerusalem,  sicut tabernacula Cedar, sicut pelles Salomonis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שְׁחוֹרָ֤ה אֲנִי֙ וְֽנָאוָ֔ה בְּנ֖וֹת יְרוּשָׁלָ֑͏ִם כְּאׇהֳלֵ֣י קֵדָ֔ר כִּירִיע֖וֹת שְׁלֹמֹֽה׃
verses.king_james: I am black, but comely, O ye daughters of Jerusalem, as the tents of Kedar, as the curtains of Solomon.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nolite me considerare quod fusca sim,  quia decoloravit me sol.  Filii matris meæ pugnaverunt contra me ;  posuerunt me custodem in vineis :  vineam meam non custodivi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַל־תִּרְא֙וּנִי֙ שֶׁאֲנִ֣י שְׁחַרְחֹ֔רֶת שֶׁשְּׁזָפַ֖תְנִי הַשָּׁ֑מֶשׁ בְּנֵ֧י אִמִּ֣י נִֽחֲרוּ־בִ֗י שָׂמֻ֙נִי֙ נֹטֵרָ֣ה אֶת־הַכְּרָמִ֔ים כַּרְמִ֥י שֶׁלִּ֖י לֹ֥א נָטָֽרְתִּי׃
verses.king_james: Look not upon me, because I am black, because the sun hath looked upon me: my mother’s children were angry with me; they made me the keeper of the vineyards; but mine own vineyard have I not kept.

verses.latin_vulgate: Indica mihi, quem diligit anima mea, ubi pascas,  ubi cubes in meridie,  ne vagari incipiam post greges sodalium tuorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַגִּ֣ידָה לִּ֗י שֶׁ֤אָֽהֲבָה֙ נַפְשִׁ֔י אֵיכָ֣ה תִרְעֶ֔ה אֵיכָ֖ה תַּרְבִּ֣יץ בַּֽצׇּהֳרָ֑יִם שַׁלָּמָ֤ה אֶֽהְיֶה֙ כְּעֹ֣טְיָ֔ה עַ֖ל עֶדְרֵ֥י חֲבֵרֶֽיךָ׃
verses.king_james: Tell me, O thou whom my soul loveth, where thou feedest, where thou makest thy flock to rest at noon: for why should I be as one that turneth aside by the flocks of thy companions?

verses.latin_vulgate: Si ignoras te, o pulcherrima inter mulieres,  egredere, et abi post vestigia gregum,  et pasce hædos tuos juxta tabernacula pastorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִם־לֹ֤א תֵֽדְעִי֙ לָ֔ךְ הַיָּפָ֖ה בַּנָּשִׁ֑ים צְֽאִי־לָ֞ךְ בְּעִקְבֵ֣י הַצֹּ֗אן וּרְעִי֙ אֶת־גְּדִיֹּתַ֔יִךְ עַ֖ל מִשְׁכְּנ֥וֹת הָרֹעִֽים׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: If thou know not, O thou fairest among women, go thy way forth by the footsteps of the flock, and feed thy kids beside the shepherds’ tents.

verses.latin_vulgate: Equitatui meo in curribus Pharaonis  assimilavi te, amica mea.
verses.king_james: I have compared thee, O my love, to a company of horses in Pharaoh’s chariots.

verses.latin_vulgate: Pulchræ sunt genæ tuæ sicut turturis ;  collum tuum sicut monilia.
verses.king_james: Thy cheeks are comely with rows of jewels, thy neck with chains of gold.

verses.latin_vulgate: Murenulas aureas faciemus tibi,  vermiculatas argento.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תּוֹרֵ֤י זָהָב֙ נַֽעֲשֶׂה־לָּ֔ךְ עִ֖ם נְקֻדּ֥וֹת הַכָּֽסֶף׃
verses.king_james: We will make thee borders of gold with studs of silver.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dum esset rex in accubitu suo,  nardus mea dedit odorem suum.
verses.king_james: While the king sitteth at his table, my spikenard sendeth forth the smell thereof.

verses.latin_vulgate: Fasciculus myrrhæ dilectus meus mihi ;  inter ubera mea commorabitur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: צְר֨וֹר הַמֹּ֤ר ׀ דּוֹדִי֙ לִ֔י בֵּ֥ין שָׁדַ֖י יָלִֽין׃
verses.king_james: A bundle of myrrh is my wellbeloved unto me; he shall lie all night betwixt my breasts.

verses.latin_vulgate: Botrus cypri dilectus meus mihi  in vineis Engaddi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֶשְׁכֹּ֨ל הַכֹּ֤פֶר ׀ דּוֹדִי֙ לִ֔י בְּכַרְמֵ֖י עֵ֥ין גֶּֽדִי׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: My beloved is unto me as a cluster of camphire in the vineyards of En–gedi.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ecce tu pulchra es, amica mea ! ecce tu pulchra es !  Oculi tui columbarum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הִנָּ֤ךְ יָפָה֙ רַעְיָתִ֔י הִנָּ֥ךְ יָפָ֖ה עֵינַ֥יִךְ יוֹנִֽים׃
verses.king_james: Behold, thou art fair, my love; behold, thou art fair; thou hast doves’ eyes.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ecce tu pulcher es, dilecte mi, et decorus !  Lectulus noster floridus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הִנְּךָ֨ יָפֶ֤ה דוֹדִי֙ אַ֣ף נָעִ֔ים אַף־עַרְשֵׂ֖נוּ רַעֲנָנָֽה׃
verses.king_james: Behold, thou art fair, my beloved, yea, pleasant: also our bed is green.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tigna domorum nostrarum cedrina,  laquearia nostra cypressina.
verses.king_james: The beams of our house are cedar, and our rafters of fir.
